name: What siding details prevent mold behind the walls?

mainEntity.name: How do contractors reduce the risk of mold when installing new siding?
mainEntity.text: How do contractors reduce the risk of mold when installing new siding?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mold prevention starts with preventing chronic moisture intrusion. Contractors should use correct flashing at openings, maintain proper clearances, integrate the weather barrier correctly, and avoid sealing water into the wall cavity with incompatible materials or careless caulking. In many homes, mold begins because small leaks go unnoticed behind the siding for years. Coastal areas are especially vulnerable because fog and wind-driven rain create frequent wetting events. If a project reveals mold or staining, the contractor should determine whether the source is an active leak, an old event, or both before covering anything up. Homeowners should ask how the wall assembly will drain and dry, not just how the finished exterior will look.
acceptedAnswer.description: Mold prevention depends on proper flashing, drainage, drying space, air sealing, and keeping water out of the wall cavity. The best installations avoid trapping moisture behind the siding and use materials that allow the wall to dry if minor wetting occurs.
